name: Fighter JSON — PART 1: features block
note: Paste this into your 'features' object in your homebrew JSON file. All shared + subclass features included.

fighter_fighting_style.name: Fighter's Fighting Style
fighter_fighting_style.description: You adopt a particular style of fighting as your specialty. Choose one of the following options. You can't take the same Fighting Style option more than once, even if you get to choose again.

fighting_style_archery.name: Archery
fighting_style_archery.description: You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls you make with ranged weapons.

fighting_style_blind_fighting.name: Blind Fighting
fighting_style_blind_fighting.description: You have blindsight with a range of 3 meters. Within that range, you can effectively see anything that isn't behind total cover, even if you're blinded or in darkness. Moreover, you can see an invisible creature within that range, unless the creature successfully hides from you.

fighting_style_defense.name: Defense
fighting_style_defense.description: While you are wearing armor, you gain a +1 bonus to AC.

fighting_style_dueling.name: Dueling
fighting_style_dueling.description: When you are wielding a melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons, you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls with that weapon.

fighting_style_great_weapon_fighting.name: Great Weapon Fighting
fighting_style_great_weapon_fighting.description: When you roll a 1 or 2 on a damage die for an attack you make with a melee weapon that you are wielding with two hands, you can reroll the die and must use the new roll, even if the new roll is a 1 or a 2. The weapon must have the two-handed or versatile property for you to gain this benefit.

fighting_style_interception.name: Interception
fighting_style_interception.description: When a creature you can see hits a target, other than you, within your shield's or weapon's range with an attack, you can use your reaction to reduce the damage the target takes by 1d10 + your proficiency bonus (to a minimum of 0 damage). You must be wielding a shield or a simple or martial weapon to use this reaction.

fighting_style_protection.name: Protection
fighting_style_protection.description: When a creature you can see attacks a target other than you that is within your shield's range, you can use your reaction to impose disadvantage on the attack roll. You must be wielding a shield.

fighting_style_superior_technique.name: Superior Technique
fighting_style_superior_technique.description: You learn one maneuver of your choice from among those available to the Battle Master archetype. If a maneuver you use requires your target to make a saving throw to resist the maneuver's effects, the saving throw DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice). You gain one superiority die, which is a d6. This die is used to fuel your maneuvers. A superiority die is expended when you use it. You regain your expended superiority dice when you finish a short or long rest.

fighting_style_thrown_weapon.name: Thrown Weapon Fighting
fighting_style_thrown_weapon.description: You can draw a weapon that has the thrown property as part of the attack you make with the weapon. In addition, when you hit with a ranged attack using a thrown weapon, you gain a +2 bonus to the damage roll.

fighting_style_two_weapon.name: Two-Weapon Fighting
fighting_style_two_weapon.description: When you engage in two-weapon fighting, you can add your ability modifier to the damage of the second attack.

fighting_style_unarmed.name: Unarmed Fighting
fighting_style_unarmed.description: Your unarmed strikes can deal bludgeoning damage equal to 1d6 + your Strength modifier on a hit. If you aren't wielding any weapons or a shield when you make the attack roll, the d6 becomes a d8. At the start of each of your turns, you can deal 1d4 bludgeoning damage to one creature grappled by you.

fighter_second_wind.name: Second Wind
fighter_second_wind.description: You have a limited well of stamina that you can draw on to protect yourself from harm. On your turn, you can use a bonus action to regain hit points equal to 1d10 + your fighter level. Once you use this feature, you must finish a short or long rest before you can use it again.

fighter_action_surge.name: Action Surge
fighter_action_surge.description: You can push yourself beyond your normal limits for a moment. On your turn, you can take one additional action, except the Magic action. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a Short or Long Rest. Starting at level 17, you can use it twice before a rest but only once on a turn.

fighter_action_surge_2.name: Action Surge (Two Uses)
fighter_action_surge_2.description: At 17th level, you can use Action Surge twice before a rest, but only once on the same turn.

fighter_signature_maneuver.name: Signature Maneuver
fighter_signature_maneuver.description:
Starting at 2nd level, your training has had you perfect a fighting maneuver. You learn a maneuver that is fueled by a special die called a superiority die. Choose a maneuver from the maneuver list that you can use with a d6 as your superiority die. You can use this maneuver once per initiative.

Maneuver save DC = 8 + Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice) + Proficiency Bonus

fighter_maneuvers.name: Maneuvers
fighter_maneuvers.description:
You have access to the following maneuvers, fueled by superiority dice. A superiority die is expended when you use it. You regain all expended superiority dice when you enter initiative.

Maneuver save DC = 8 + Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice) + Proficiency Bonus

maneuver_ambush.name: Ambush
maneuver_ambush.description: When you make a Dexterity (Stealth) check or an initiative roll, you can expend one superiority die and add the die to the roll, provided you aren't incapacitated.

maneuver_bait_and_switch.name: Bait and Switch
maneuver_bait_and_switch.description: When you're within 1.5 meters of a creature on your turn, you can expend one superiority die and switch places with that creature, provided you spend at least 1.5 meters of movement and the creature is willing and isn't incapacitated. This movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks. Roll the superiority die. Until the start of your next turn, you or the other creature (your choice) gains a bonus to AC equal to the number rolled.

maneuver_brace.name: Brace
maneuver_brace.description: When a creature you can see moves into the reach you have with the melee weapon you're wielding, you can use your reaction to expend one superiority die and make one attack against the creature, using that weapon. If the attack hits, add the superiority die to the weapon's damage roll.

maneuver_commanders_strike.name: Commander's Strike
maneuver_commanders_strike.description: When you take the Attack action on your turn, you can forgo one of your attacks and use a bonus action to direct one of your companions to strike. Choose a friendly creature who can see or hear you and expend one superiority die. That creature can immediately use its reaction to make one weapon attack, adding the superiority die to the attack's damage roll.

maneuver_commanding_presence.name: Commanding Presence
maneuver_commanding_presence.description: When you make a Charisma (Intimidation), a Charisma (Performance), or a Charisma (Persuasion) check, you can expend one superiority die and add the superiority die to the ability check.

maneuver_disarming_attack.name: Disarming Attack
maneuver_disarming_attack.description: When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can expend one superiority die to attempt to disarm the target, forcing it to drop one item of your choice that it's holding. You add the superiority die to the attack's damage roll, and the target must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, it drops the object you choose. The object lands at its feet.

maneuver_distracting_strike.name: Distracting Strike
maneuver_distracting_strike.description: When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can expend one superiority die to distract the creature, giving your allies an opening. You add the superiority die to the attack's damage roll. The next attack roll against the target by an attacker other than you has advantage if the attack is made before the start of your next turn.

maneuver_evasive_footwork.name: Evasive Footwork
maneuver_evasive_footwork.description: When you move, you can expend one superiority die, rolling the die and adding the number rolled to your AC until you stop moving.

maneuver_feinting_attack.name: Feinting Attack
maneuver_feinting_attack.description: You can expend one superiority die and use a bonus action on your turn to feint, choosing one creature within 1.5 meters of you as your target. You have advantage on your next attack roll against that creature this turn. If that attack hits, add the superiority die to the attack's damage roll.

maneuver_goading_attack.name: Goading Attack
maneuver_goading_attack.description: When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can expend one superiority die to attempt to goad the target into attacking you. You add the superiority die to the attack's damage roll, and the target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the target has disadvantage on all attack rolls against targets other than you until the end of your next turn.

maneuver_grappling_strike.name: Grappling Strike
maneuver_grappling_strike.description: Immediately after you hit a creature with a melee attack on your turn, you can expend one superiority die and then try to grapple the target as a bonus action. Add the superiority die to your Strength (Athletics) check.

maneuver_lunging_attack.name: Lunging Attack
maneuver_lunging_attack.description: When you make a melee weapon attack on your turn, you can expend one superiority die to increase your reach for that attack by 1.5 meters. If you hit, you add the superiority die to the attack's damage roll.

maneuver_maneuvering_attack.name: Maneuvering Attack
maneuver_maneuvering_attack.description: When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can expend one superiority die to maneuver one of your comrades into a more advantageous position. You add the superiority die to the attack's damage roll, and you choose a friendly creature who can see or hear you. That creature can use its reaction to move up to half its speed without provoking opportunity attacks from the target of your attack.

maneuver_menacing_attack.name: Menacing Attack
maneuver_menacing_attack.description: When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can expend one superiority die to attempt to frighten the target. You add the superiority die to the attack's damage roll, and the target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, it is frightened of you until the end of your next turn.

maneuver_parry.name: Parry
maneuver_parry.description: When another creature damages you with a melee attack, you can use your reaction and expend one superiority die to reduce the damage by the number you roll on your superiority die + your Dexterity modifier.

maneuver_precision_attack.name: Precision Attack
maneuver_precision_attack.description: When you make a weapon attack roll against a creature, you can expend one superiority die to add it to the roll. You can use this maneuver before or after making the attack roll, but before any effects of the attack are applied.

maneuver_pushing_attack.name: Pushing Attack
maneuver_pushing_attack.description: When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can expend one superiority die to attempt to drive the target back. You add the superiority die to the attack's damage roll, and if the target is Large or smaller, it must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, you push the target up to 4.5 meters away from you.

maneuver_quick_toss.name: Quick Toss
maneuver_quick_toss.description: As a bonus action, you can expend one superiority die and make a ranged attack with a weapon that has the thrown property. You can draw the weapon as part of making this attack. If you hit, add the superiority die to the weapon's damage roll.

maneuver_rally.name: Rally
maneuver_rally.description: On your turn, you can use a bonus action and expend one superiority die to bolster the resolve of one of your companions. When you do so, choose a friendly creature who can see or hear you. That creature gains temporary hit points equal to the superiority die roll + your Charisma modifier.

maneuver_riposte.name: Riposte
maneuver_riposte.description: When a creature misses you with a melee attack, you can use your reaction and expend one superiority die to make a melee weapon attack against the creature. If you hit, you add the superiority die to the attack's damage roll.

maneuver_sweeping_attack.name: Sweeping Attack
maneuver_sweeping_attack.description: When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can expend one superiority die to attempt to damage another creature with the same attack. Choose another creature within 1.5 meters of the original target and within your reach. If the original attack roll would hit the second creature, it takes damage equal to the number you roll on your superiority die. The damage is of the same type dealt by the original attack.

maneuver_tactical_assessment.name: Tactical Assessment
maneuver_tactical_assessment.description: When you make an Intelligence (Investigation), an Intelligence (History), or a Wisdom (Insight) check, you can expend one superiority die and add the superiority die to the ability check.

maneuver_trip_attack.name: Trip Attack
maneuver_trip_attack.description: When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can expend one superiority die to attempt to knock the target down. You add the superiority die to the attack's damage roll, and if the target is Large or smaller, it must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, you knock the target prone.

fighter_martial_versatility.name: Martial Versatility
fighter_martial_versatility.description: Whenever you reach a level in this class that grants the Ability Score Improvement feature, you can do one of the following: Replace a fighting style you know with another fighting style available to fighters. If you know any maneuvers from the Battle Master archetype, you can replace one maneuver you know with a different maneuver.

fighter_asi.name: Fighter's Ability Score Improvement
fighter_asi.description: When you reach this level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a feat.

fighter_extra_attack.name: Fighter's Extra Attack
fighter_extra_attack.description: Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn. The number of attacks increases to three when you reach 11th level in this class and to four when you reach 20th level in this class.

fighter_extra_attack_2.name: Extra Attack (2)
fighter_extra_attack_2.description: At 11th level, you can attack three times whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

fighter_extra_attack_3.name: Extra Attack (3)
fighter_extra_attack_3.description: At 20th level, you can attack four times whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

fighter_indomitable.name: Indomitable
fighter_indomitable.description: Beginning at 9th level, when you are asked to roll a saving throw, you can choose to succeed instead of rolling the save by spending an available charge of this feature. You can use this feature twice between long rests starting at 13th level and three times between long rests starting at 17th level.

fighter_indomitable_2.name: Indomitable (Two Uses)
fighter_indomitable_2.description: At 13th level, you can use Indomitable twice between long rests.

fighter_indomitable_3.name: Indomitable (Three Uses)
fighter_indomitable_3.description: At 17th level, you can use Indomitable three times between long rests.

arcane_archer_lore.name: Arcane Archer Lore
arcane_archer_lore.description: At 3rd level, you learn magical theory or some of the secrets of history. You choose to gain proficiency in either the Arcana or the History skill, and you choose to learn either the prestidigitation or the thaumaturgy cantrip.

arcane_archer_shot.name: Arcane Shot
arcane_archer_shot.description:
At 3rd level, you learn to unleash special magical effects with some of your shots. When you gain this feature, you learn three Arcane Shot options of your choice. Once per turn when you fire an arrow from a shortbow or longbow as part of the Attack action, you can apply one of your Arcane Shot options to that arrow. You have two uses of this ability, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest. You gain an additional Arcane Shot option at 7th, 10th, 15th, and 18th level.

Arcane Shot save DC = 8 + Intelligence modifier + Proficiency Bonus

**Banishing Arrow.** The creature hit must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be banished. While banished, the target's speed is 0 and it is incapacitated. At the end of its next turn, the target reappears. (18th level: also takes 2d6 force damage.)

**Beguiling Arrow.** The creature hit takes an extra 2d6 psychic damage. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by a chosen ally until the start of your next turn. (18th level: 4d6 psychic.)

**Bursting Arrow.** Immediately after the arrow hits, the target and all creatures within 10 feet take 2d6 force damage. (18th level: 4d6 force.)

**Enfeebling Arrow.** The creature hit takes an extra 2d6 necrotic damage and must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or have its weapon attack damage halved until the start of your next turn. (18th level: 4d6 necrotic.)

**Grasping Arrow.** The creature hit takes an extra 2d6 poison damage, its speed is reduced by 10 feet, and it takes 2d6 slashing damage the first time on each turn it moves 1 foot or more without teleporting. The brambles last for 1 minute or until removed with a Strength (Athletics) check. (18th level: 4d6 each.)

**Piercing Arrow.** No attack roll. The arrow shoots forward in a 1-foot-wide, 30-foot-long line. Each creature in the line makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking arrow damage + 1d6 piercing on a fail, or half on a success. (18th level: 2d6 piercing.)

**Seeking Arrow.** No attack roll. Choose a creature you have seen in the past minute. The arrow flies toward it, ignoring cover. The target makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking arrow damage + 1d6 force on a fail (and you learn its location), or half on a success. (18th level: 2d6 force.)

**Shadow Arrow.** The creature hit takes an extra 2d6 psychic damage and must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be unable to see anything farther than 5 feet away until the start of your next turn. (18th level: 4d6 psychic.)

arcane_archer_magic_arrow.name: Magic Arrow
arcane_archer_magic_arrow.description: At 7th level, you gain the ability to infuse arrows with magic. Whenever you fire a nonmagical arrow from a shortbow or longbow, you can make it magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage. The magic fades from the arrow immediately after it hits or misses its target.

arcane_archer_curving_shot.name: Curving Shot
arcane_archer_curving_shot.description: At 7th level, you learn how to direct an errant arrow toward a new target. When you make an attack roll with a magic arrow and miss, you can use a bonus action to reroll the attack roll against a different target within 60 feet of the original target.

arcane_archer_additional_shot.name: Additional Arcane Shot Option
arcane_archer_additional_shot.description: You gain an additional Arcane Shot option of your choice. Additionally, you gain an additional use of Arcane Shot per short or long rest.

arcane_archer_ever_ready.name: Ever-Ready Shot
arcane_archer_ever_ready.description: Starting at 15th level, your magical archery is available whenever battle starts. If you roll initiative and have no uses of Arcane Shot remaining, you regain half of its uses, rounded up.

battlemaster_student_of_war.name: Student of War
battlemaster_student_of_war.description: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency with one type of artisan's tools of your choice.

battlemaster_combat_superiority.name: Combat Superiority
battlemaster_combat_superiority.description:
When you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you learn maneuvers fueled by superiority dice.

**Maneuvers.** You learn three additional maneuvers of your choice. You can use only one maneuver per attack. You learn two additional maneuvers at 7th, 10th, and 15th level. Each time you learn new maneuvers, you can also replace one maneuver you know with a different one.

**Superiority Dice.** You have four additional superiority dice (d6s). You regain all expended superiority dice when you enter initiative. You gain another superiority die at 7th level and one more at 15th level.

Maneuver save DC = 8 + Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice) + Proficiency Bonus

battlemaster_additional_maneuvers.name: Additional Maneuvers
battlemaster_additional_maneuvers.description: At 7th level, you learn two additional maneuvers of your choice. You gain this feature again at levels 10 and 15.

battlemaster_additional_superiority_die.name: Additional Superiority Die
battlemaster_additional_superiority_die.description: You gain another superiority die at 7th level. You gain this feature again at level 15.

battlemaster_know_your_enemy.name: Know Your Enemy
battlemaster_know_your_enemy.description: If you spend at least 1 minute observing or interacting with another creature outside combat, you can learn certain information about its capabilities compared to your own. The DM tells you if the creature is your equal, superior, or inferior in regard to two of the following characteristics of your choice: Strength score, Dexterity score, Constitution score, Armor Class, Current hit points, Total class levels (if any), Fighter class levels (if any).

battlemaster_improved_superiority_d8.name: Improved Combat Superiority (d8)
battlemaster_improved_superiority_d8.description: At 10th level, your superiority dice turn into d8s.

battlemaster_restless.name: Restless
battlemaster_restless.description: Once per initiative, when you use Action Surge or Second Wind you gain a Superiority Die.

battlemaster_improved_superiority_d10.name: Improved Combat Superiority (d10)
battlemaster_improved_superiority_d10.description: At 18th level, your superiority dice turn into d10s.

cavalier_bonus_proficiency.name: Cavalier's Bonus Proficiency
cavalier_bonus_proficiency.description: When you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you gain proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: Animal Handling, History, Insight, Performance, or Persuasion. Alternatively, you learn one language of your choice.

cavalier_born_to_saddle.name: Born to the Saddle
cavalier_born_to_saddle.description: Starting at 3rd level, your mastery as a rider becomes apparent. You have advantage on saving throws made to avoid falling off your mount. If you fall off your mount and descend no more than 3 meters, you can land on your feet if you're not incapacitated. Mounting or dismounting a creature costs you only 1.5 meters of movement, rather than half your speed.

cavalier_unwavering_mark.name: Unwavering Mark
cavalier_unwavering_mark.description:
Starting at 3rd level, you can menace your foes, foiling their attacks and punishing them for harming others. When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can mark the creature until the end of your next turn. While it is within 1.5 meters of you, a creature marked by you has disadvantage on any attack roll that doesn't target you.

In addition, if a creature marked by you deals damage to anyone other than you, you can make a special melee weapon attack against the marked creature as a bonus action on your next turn. You have advantage on the attack roll, and if it hits, the attack's weapon deals extra damage equal to half your fighter level.

You can make this special attack a number of times equal to your Strength modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

cavalier_warding_maneuver.name: Warding Maneuver
cavalier_warding_maneuver.description:
At 7th level, you learn to fend off strikes directed at you, your mount, or other creatures nearby. If you or a creature you can see within 1.5 meters of you is hit by an attack, you can roll 1d8 as a reaction if you're wielding a melee weapon or a shield. Roll the die, and add the number rolled to the target's AC against that attack. If the attack still hits, the target has resistance against the attack's damage.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

cavalier_hold_the_line.name: Hold the Line
cavalier_hold_the_line.description: At 10th level, you become a master of locking down your enemies. Creatures provoke an opportunity attack from you when they move 1.5 meters or more while within your reach, and if you hit a creature with an opportunity attack, the target's speed is reduced to 0 until the end of the current turn.

cavalier_ferocious_charger.name: Ferocious Charger
cavalier_ferocious_charger.description: Starting at 15th level, you can run down your foes, whether you're mounted or not. If you move at least 3 meters in a straight line right before attacking a creature and you hit it with the attack, that target must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength modifier) or be knocked prone. You can use this feature only once on each of your turns.

cavalier_vigilant_defender.name: Vigilant Defender
cavalier_vigilant_defender.description: Starting at 18th level, you respond to danger with extraordinary vigilance. In combat, you get a special reaction that you can take once on every creature's turn, except your turn. You can use this special reaction only to make an opportunity attack, and you can't use it on the same turn that you take your normal reaction.

champion_improved_critical.name: Improved Critical
champion_improved_critical.description: Beginning when you choose this archetype at 3rd level, your weapon attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20.

champion_remarkable_athlete.name: Remarkable Athlete
champion_remarkable_athlete.description: Starting at 7th level, you can add half your proficiency bonus (round up) to any Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution check you make that doesn't already use your proficiency bonus. In addition, when you make a running long jump, the distance you can cover increases by a number of feet equal to your Strength modifier.

champion_additional_fighting_style.name: Additional Fighting Style
champion_additional_fighting_style.description: At 10th level, you can choose a second option from the Fighting Style class feature.

champion_superior_critical.name: Superior Critical
champion_superior_critical.description: Starting at 15th level, your weapon attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 18–20.

champion_survivor.name: Survivor
champion_survivor.description: At 18th level, you attain the pinnacle of resilience in battle. At the start of each of your turns, you regain hit points equal to 5 + your Constitution modifier if you have no more than half of your hit points left. You don't gain this benefit if you have 0 hit points.

eldritch_knight_spellcasting.name: Eldritch Knight's Spellcasting
eldritch_knight_spellcasting.description:
When you reach 3rd level, you augment your martial prowess with the ability to cast spells. You learn two cantrips from the arcanist spell list (a third at 10th level). You know three 1st-level arcanist spells, two of which must be from the abjuration, evocation, or necromancy schools. Spells learned at 8th, 14th, and 20th level can be from any school.

Spellcasting Ability: Intelligence.
Spell save DC = 8 + Intelligence modifier + Proficiency Bonus
Spell attack modifier = Intelligence modifier + Proficiency Bonus

Only while wielding your bonded weapon do you have access to this spellcasting feature.

eldritch_knight_weapon_bond.name: Weapon Bond
eldritch_knight_weapon_bond.description:
At 3rd level, you learn a ritual that creates a magical bond between yourself and one weapon. You perform the ritual over the course of 1 hour, which can be done during a short rest. Once bonded, you can't be disarmed of that weapon unless you are incapacitated. If it is on the same plane of existence, you can summon that weapon as a bonus action on your turn, causing it to teleport instantly to your hand.

You can have up to two bonded weapons, but can summon only one at a time with your bonus action. If you attempt to bond with a third weapon, you must break the bond with one of the other two.

eldritch_knight_war_magic.name: War Magic
eldritch_knight_war_magic.description: Beginning at 7th level, when you use your action to cast a cantrip, you can make one weapon attack as a bonus action.

eldritch_knight_eldritch_strike.name: Eldritch Strike
eldritch_knight_eldritch_strike.description: At 10th level, you learn how to make your weapon strikes undercut a creature's resistance to your spells. When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, that creature has disadvantage on the next saving throw it makes against a spell you cast before the end of your next turn.

eldritch_knight_arcane_charge.name: Arcane Charge
eldritch_knight_arcane_charge.description: At 15th level, you gain the ability to teleport up to 9 meters to an unoccupied space you can see when you use your Action Surge. You can teleport before or after the additional action.

eldritch_knight_improved_war_magic.name: Improved War Magic
eldritch_knight_improved_war_magic.description: Starting at 18th level, when you use your action to cast a spell, you can make one weapon attack as a bonus action.

legion_banneret_knighthood.name: Knighthood
legion_banneret_knighthood.description: The forces of Manyhal are organized in legions, each one a family of 500 members. The highest members of each legion are captains, commanders and knights. The lowest form of nobility, knighthood gives way to honor, wealth, respect and prosperity.

legion_banneret_rallying.name: Rallying
legion_banneret_rallying.description:
When you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you learn how to inspire your allies to fight on past their injuries.

When you use your Second Wind feature, you can choose up to a number of creatures equal to your proficiency bonus within 18 meters of you that are your allies. Each one regains hit points equal to your fighter level + your Charisma modifier, provided that the creature can see or hear you.

Additionally, once per turn when you take the Attack action, you can forgo one of your attacks to command one of your companions within your banner's range. That creature can immediately use its reaction to take an action. If this action would be an attack or spell, it must follow the 'Opportunity Attack' rules. Instead of an action, the ally may instead choose to use their movement.

legion_banneret_knights_standard.name: Knight's Standard
legion_banneret_knights_standard.description: You carry a banner of your liege's house. As an action you may plant the banner on the ground, bolstering your allies' morale. Allies within 9 meters of your banner have advantage against being frightened or disarmed.

legion_banneret_royal_envoy.name: Royal Envoy
legion_banneret_royal_envoy.description:
A Legionnaire serves as an extension of the king. At 7th level, you gain expertise with a Charisma skill with which you are already proficient. Any roll you make with a Charisma skill — you can treat a d20 roll of 9 or lower as a 10.

Additionally, any ally within your banner's range has advantage against being charmed.

legion_banneret_bulwark.name: Bulwark
legion_banneret_bulwark.description: At 10th level, you can extend the benefit of your Indomitable feature to your allies. When you decide to use Indomitable to reroll an Intelligence, a Wisdom, or a Charisma saving throw and you aren't incapacitated, you can choose one ally within 18 meters of you that also failed its saving throw against the same effect. If your ally can see or hear you, it can reroll its saving throw and must use the new roll.

legion_banneret_legion_commander.name: Legion Commander
legion_banneret_legion_commander.description: Starting at 15th level, your banner's range increases from 9 to 18 meters. Additionally, any creature within your banner's range gains +1 AC and 1.5 meters of movement.

legion_banneret_uncheckable.name: Uncheckable
legion_banneret_uncheckable.description: Starting at 18th level, when two allies or more are conscious and inside your banner's range, neither you nor them can be flanked. Additionally, you become immune to the Dazed, Grappled, Prone and Stunned conditions.

rune_knight_bonus_proficiencies.name: Rune Knight's Bonus Proficiencies
rune_knight_bonus_proficiencies.description: You gain proficiency with smith's tools, and you gain proficiency in Soddish.

rune_knight_rune_carver.name: Rune Carver
rune_knight_rune_carver.description:
You can use magic runes to enhance your gear. You learn two runes of your choice (3 at 7th, 4 at 10th, 5 at 15th level). Whenever you finish a long rest, you can inscribe a different rune onto a number of objects equal to the number of runes you know. An object must be a weapon, armor, shield, jewelry, or something you can wear or hold.

Rune Magic save DC = 8 + proficiency bonus + Constitution modifier

**Cloud Rune.** Advantage on Sleight of Hand and Deception checks. Reaction: redirect an attack roll to a different creature within 9m. Recharges on short/long rest.

**Fire Rune.** Double proficiency bonus for tool checks. On hit: target takes 2d6 fire damage and must make a Strength save or be restrained for 1 minute (taking 2d6 fire at start of each turn). Recharges on short/long rest.

**Frost Rune.** Advantage on Animal Handling and Intimidation checks. Bonus action: gain +2 to Strength and Constitution checks and saves for 10 minutes. Recharges on short/long rest.

**Hill Rune (7th+).** Advantage on saves against poison; resistance to poison damage. Bonus action: resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing for 1 minute. Recharges on short/long rest.

**Stone Rune.** Advantage on Insight checks; darkvision 36m. Reaction: force a creature ending its turn within 9m to make a Wisdom save or be charmed (speed 0, incapacitated) for 1 minute. Recharges on short/long rest.

**Storm Rune (7th+).** Advantage on Arcana checks; can't be surprised. Bonus action: enter prophetic state for 1 minute — use reaction to give advantage or disadvantage on any attack roll, save, or ability check within 9m. Recharges on short/long rest.

rune_knight_giants_might.name: Giant's Might
rune_knight_giants_might.description:
You have learned how to imbue yourself with the might of giants. As a bonus action, you magically gain the following benefits for 1 minute: If you are smaller than Large, you become Large (if space allows). You have advantage on Strength checks and Strength saving throws. Once on each of your turns, one of your attacks with a weapon or an unarmed strike can deal an extra 1d6 damage to a target on a hit.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

rune_knight_runic_shield.name: Runic Shield
rune_knight_runic_shield.description:
You learn to invoke your rune magic to protect your allies. When another creature you can see within 9 meters of you is hit by an attack roll, you can use your reaction to force the attacker to reroll the d20 and use the new roll.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

rune_knight_additional_rune.name: Additional Rune Known
rune_knight_additional_rune.description: You learn an additional Rune from your Rune Carver feature.

rune_knight_great_stature.name: Great Stature
rune_knight_great_stature.description: At 10th level, the magic of your runes permanently alters you. When you gain this feature, roll 3d4. You grow a number of centimeters in height equal to the roll. Moreover, the extra damage you deal with the Giant's Might feature increases to 1d8.

rune_knight_master_of_runes.name: Master of Runes
rune_knight_master_of_runes.description: At 15th level, you can invoke each rune you know from your Rune Carver feature twice, rather than once, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

rune_knight_runic_juggernaut.name: Runic Juggernaut
rune_knight_runic_juggernaut.description: At 18th level, you learn how to amplify your rune-powered transformation. The extra damage you deal with the Giant's Might feature increases to 1d10. Moreover, when you use that feature, your size can increase to Huge, and while you are that size, your reach increases by 1.5 meters.

subclasses.name: Arcane Archer
subclasses.description: An Arcane Archer studies a unique elven method of archery that weaves magic into attacks to produce supernatural effects. Arcane Archers are some of their most elite warriors among the elves.

subclasses.name: Battle Master
subclasses.description: Those who emulate the archetypal Battle Master employ martial techniques passed down through generations. A Battle Master might be a veteran who taught others the arts of war, a student of a master fighter, or a warrior who learned to combine brute force with tactical cunning.

subclasses.name: Cavalier
subclasses.description: The archetypal Cavalier excels at mounted combat. Usually born among the nobility and raised at court, a Cavalier is equally at home leading a cavalry charge or exchanging repartee at a state dinner.

subclasses.name: Champion
subclasses.description: The archetypal Champion focuses on the development of raw physical power honed to deadly perfection. Those who model themselves on this archetype combine rigorous training with physical excellence to deal devastating blows.

subclasses.name: Eldritch Knight
subclasses.description: The archetypal Eldritch Knight combines the martial mastery common to all fighters with a careful study of magic. Eldritch Knights use magical techniques similar to those practiced by wizards.

subclasses.name: Legion Banneret
subclasses.description: A Legion Banneret is a knight of the highest order, serving as an extension of the king's will. They inspire their allies through their presence, their banner, and their unbreakable resolve.

subclasses.name: Rune Knight
subclasses.description: Rune Knights enhance their martial prowess using the supernatural power of runes, an ancient practice that originated with giants. Rune cutters can be found among any family of giants, and you likely learned your methods first or second hand from such a mystical artisan.
